indications.name: Moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease confirmed by endoscopy, colonoscopy, or imaging (CT/MRI enterography)
Failure, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one conventional therapy (corticosteroids such as prednisone, AND at least one immunomodulator: azathioprine, 6-mercaptopurine, or methotrexate)
Negative tuberculosis test (TST or IGRA) within 6 months prior to initiation
Hepatitis B surface antigen screening completed and documented
Patient is not currently pregnant or breastfeeding (or risk/benefit discussed and documented)
indications.clinical_guideline: ECCO Guidelines for Crohn's Disease 2023; ACG Clinical Guideline 2021

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis confirmed by rheumatologist
DAS28 score > 3.2 or CDAI score > 10 documented
Failure or inadequate response to at least two DMARDs including methotrexate
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening within 6 months

indications.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
HbA1c >= 7.5% documented within the last 3 months
Failure, inadequate response, or documented contraindication to metformin at maximum tolerated dose for at least 3 months
BMI >= 27 kg/m2 documented within the last 12 months
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2 documented
indications.clinical_guideline: ADA Standards of Medical Care in Diabetes 2024; AACE/ACE Algorithm 2023

indications.name: Chronic obesity (BMI >= 30 or >= 27 with comorbidity)
required_criteria: BMI >= 30 kg/m2, OR BMI >= 27 kg/m2 with at least one weight-related comorbidity (hypertension, type 2 diabetes, dyslipidemia, or obstructive sleep apnea)
Documented failure of at least one structured lifestyle intervention program (minimum 16 weeks with dietary counseling and physical activity) with less than 5% body weight loss
No personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma or MEN2 syndrome
No active pancreatitis or history of pancreatitis
No concurrent use of other GLP-1 receptor agonists
Prescriber is endocrinologist, obesity medicine specialist, or PCP with documented obesity management training
indications.clinical_guideline: AHA/ACC/TOS Obesity Guideline 2023; AACE Obesity Clinical Practice Guidelines 2022

indications.name: Non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) — first-line monotherapy
required_criteria: Histologically or cytologically confirmed NSCLC with pathology report on file
PD-L1 tumor proportion score (TPS) >= 50% confirmed by FDA-approved diagnostic test (22C3 antibody)
EGFR mutation status documented as negative (wildtype)
ALK rearrangement status documented as negative
ECOG performance status 0 or 1 documented within 30 days
No active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive therapy within 2 years
No prior systemic chemotherapy for metastatic NSCLC
Prescriber is board-certified oncologist
indications.clinical_guideline: NCCN Clinical Practice Guidelines: NSCLC v2.2024; ASCO 2023 Update

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis confirmed by board-certified dermatologist
EASI score >= 16 OR IGA score >= 3 documented within last 6 months
Inadequate response or intolerance to at least two prescription-strength topical corticosteroids of different potency classes used for at least 4 weeks each
Inadequate response, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one systemic immunosuppressant (cyclosporine, methotrexate, azathioprine, or mycophenolate mofetil)
Patient age >= 6 years
indications.clinical_guideline: AAD-NPF Guidelines for Atopic Dermatitis 2023; EADV Guidelines 2022

indications.name: Stroke prevention in non-valvular atrial fibrillation
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of non-valvular atrial fibrillation (ECG or Holter documentation)
CHA2DS2-VASc score >= 2 for men, >= 3 for women — documented calculation on file

One of: (a) documented contraindication to warfarin, (b) INR instability on warfarin with TTR < 60% over 6 months, (c) history of warfarin-related bleeding, (d) patient preference with shared decision-making documented
Renal function: CrCl >= 15 mL/min documented
No concurrent use of other anticoagulants without justification
No mechanical heart valve prosthesis
indications.clinical_guideline: AHA/ACC/HRS Guideline for AF 2023; CHEST Antithrombotic Therapy Guidelines 2022

indications.name: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (HFrEF)
required_criteria: Diagnosis of chronic heart failure with LVEF <= 40% documented by echocardiogram within 12 months
NYHA functional class II or III symptoms documented
Current treatment with a stable dose of ACE inhibitor OR ARB for at least 4 weeks
Systolic BP >= 100 mmHg at time of initiation
eGFR >= 30 mL/min/1.73m2 within 3 months
Potassium < 5.4 mEq/L within 3 months
No history of angioedema related to ACE inhibitor or ARB
Prescriber is cardiologist or heart failure specialist
indications.clinical_guideline: ACC/AHA/HFSA 2022 Heart Failure Guideline; ESC 2021 Heart Failure Guidelines

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease — biologic-experienced
required_criteria: Confirmed moderate-to-severe Crohn's disease by endoscopy or imaging
Failure, loss of response, or intolerance to at least one TNF-alpha inhibitor (adalimumab, infliximab, or certolizumab pegol)
Failure or inadequate response to conventional therapies
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening within 6 months
No active serious infection at time of initiation
Prescriber is gastroenterologist
indications.clinical_guideline: ECCO Guidelines 2023; ACG 2021 Biologic Therapy Guidance

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis by endoscopy
Mayo endoscopic score >= 2 documented
Failure, intolerance, or contraindication to at least one TNF-alpha inhibitor
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening
Absolute lymphocyte count >= 750 cells/mm3
Hemoglobin >= 8 g/dL
Prescriber is gastroenterologist
indications.clinical_guideline: ACG 2022 UC Guidelines; ECCO UC Consensus 2022

indications.name: Chronic kidney disease to reduce eGFR decline
required_criteria: Confirmed CKD stage 3a or higher
eGFR 25-75 mL/min/1.73m2 within last 3 months
UACR >= 200 mg/g documented (or Type 2 diabetes diagnosis)
No type 1 diabetes
No current dialysis or kidney transplant
Prescriber is nephrologist, endocrinologist, or PCP with CKD management experience
indications.clinical_guideline: KDIGO 2022 CKD Guidelines; ADA 2024 Standards of Care

indications.name: Relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (RRMS)
required_criteria: Confirmed RRMS diagnosis per McDonald Criteria 2017, confirmed by neurologist
MRI evidence of active disease within last 12 months
Failure or inadequate response to at least one first-line DMT for minimum 6 months
JC virus antibody index documented prior to initiation
Enrolled in TOUCH Prescribing Program (FDA REMS requirement)
No concurrent use of other immunosuppressive therapies
Prescriber is board-certified neurologist enrolled in TOUCH program
indications.clinical_guideline: AAN Practice Guidelines for MS 2022; ECTRIMS/EAN MS Treatment Guidelines 2021

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe endometriosis-associated pain
required_criteria: Diagnosis of endometriosis confirmed by laparoscopy or laparotomy with histological confirmation, OR confirmed by imaging (ultrasound or MRI) demonstrating endometriomas or deep infiltrating endometriosis
Moderate-to-severe dysmenorrhea OR non-menstrual pelvic pain documented with pain scoring (NRS >= 4/10) within last 3 months

Failure or inadequate response to at least one first-line therapy: combined oral contraceptives (minimum 3 months), progestins (norethindrone or medroxyprogesterone, minimum 3 months), or NSAIDs at therapeutic doses
Patient is premenopausal and not currently pregnant
Bone density (DEXA scan) documented at baseline for duration > 6 months or history of osteoporosis
No severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C)
Osteoporosis or significant risk factors for bone loss discussed and documented
Prescriber is gynecologist, reproductive endocrinologist, or OB/GYN with endometriosis management experience
indications.clinical_guideline: ACOG Practice Bulletin No. 114: Endometriosis 2023; ESHRE Endometriosis Guideline 2022

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis confirmed by dermatologist
BSA involvement >= 10% OR DLQI score >= 10 OR PASI score >= 10 documented
Inadequate response, contraindication, or intolerance to at least two conventional systemic agents (methotrexate, cyclosporine, or acitretin) OR phototherapy
Failure or inadequate response to at least one other biologic (TNF-alpha inhibitor or IL-17 inhibitor) if biologics previously tried
Negative TB test within 6 months prior to initiation
Hepatitis B and C screening completed
Prescriber is dermatologist
indications.clinical_guideline: AAD-NPF Guidelines for Psoriasis 2020; Joint AAD-NPF Biologic Guidance 2023

indications.name: Active psoriatic arthritis
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of psoriatic arthritis by rheumatologist, meeting CASPAR criteria
Active disease: >= 3 tender joints AND >= 3 swollen joints, OR active enthesitis, OR active dactylitis documented
Failure or inadequate response to at least one conventional DMARD (methotrexate, leflunomide, or sulfasalazine) for minimum 3 months at therapeutic dose
Negative TB test and hepatitis B screening within 6 months
Prescriber is rheumatologist
indications.clinical_guideline: ACR/NPF Psoriatic Arthritis Guidelines 2021; EULAR PsA Recommendations 2019

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis — DMARD-inadequate response
required_criteria: Confirmed moderate-to-severe rheumatoid arthritis by rheumatologist
DAS28 score > 3.2 OR CDAI > 10 OR SDAI > 11 documented within 3 months
Failure or inadequate response to at least one conventional DMARD (methotrexate at >= 15mg/week for >= 3 months) AND at least one biologic DMARD (TNF inhibitor or IL-6 inhibitor)
Negative TB test within 6 months prior to initiation
Absolute lymphocyte count >= 500 cells/mm3 and absolute neutrophil count >= 1000 cells/mm3
Hemoglobin >= 8 g/dL
No current active, serious, or chronic infection
No current or prior malignancy except non-melanoma skin cancer
Not recommended in patients > 65 years, current smokers, or those with cardiovascular risk factors without documented benefit/risk discussion
Prescriber is board-certified rheumatologist; FDA REMS enrollment required
indications.clinical_guideline: ACR RA Guidelines 2021; FDA REMS requirements for JAK inhibitors 2022

indications.name: Active ankylosing spondylitis (radiographic axial spondyloarthritis)
required_criteria: Confirmed diagnosis of ankylosing spondylitis meeting modified New York Criteria, confirmed by rheumatologist
Active disease: BASDAI score >= 4 OR ASDAS score >= 2.1 documented
MRI or X-ray evidence of sacroiliitis or spinal inflammation documented within 12 months
Failure or inadequate response to at least two NSAIDs at maximum tolerated dose for minimum 4 weeks each
For patients with contraindication to NSAIDs: documented intolerance or contraindication
Negative TB test within 6 months prior to initiation
Hepatitis B screening completed
Prescriber is rheumatologist
indications.clinical_guideline: ACR/SAA/SPARTAN Axial Spondyloarthritis Guidelines 2019; ASAS-EULAR AS Recommendations 2022

indications.name: Chronic insomnia disorder
required_criteria: Diagnosis of chronic insomnia disorder (difficulty >= 3 nights/week for >= 3 months)
Documented failure of cognitive behavioral therapy for insomnia (CBT-I) or documented barrier to access
Failure or inadequate response to at least two first-line agents: zolpidem, eszopiclone, or zaleplon
No narcolepsy diagnosis
Prescriber is sleep medicine specialist, psychiatrist, or PCP with documented sleep medicine experience
indications.clinical_guideline: AASM Clinical Practice Guideline for Insomnia 2023; ACP Insomnia Management Guidelines 2021

indications.name: Moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis
required_criteria: Diagnosis of moderate-to-severe plaque psoriasis confirmed by dermatologist
BSA involvement >= 10% OR DLQI score >= 10 OR PASI score >= 10 documented
Inadequate response or intolerance to at least two topical therapies used for minimum 3 months
Inadequate response, contraindication, or intolerance to at least one conventional systemic agent or phototherapy
No severe renal impairment (CrCl < 30 mL/min requires dose reduction)
Prescriber is dermatologist or rheumatologist
indications.clinical_guideline: AAD-NPF Guidelines for Psoriasis 2020; Joint AAD-NPF 2023 Biologic Guidance
